mnemonics: FCFS: First Come, First Serve - like a queue at a coffee shop
SJF: Shortest Job First - handle quick tasks first to reduce waiting time
Priority Scheduling: VIPs (high priority) get served first
keyPoints: Thread scheduling decides which thread runs next on the CPU
Scheduling algorithms aim to maximize CPU utilization and minimize waiting time
Common algorithms include FCFS, SJF, Priority Scheduling, and Round Robin
commonMistakes: Assuming SJF is always optimal (it can cause starvation of longer threads)
Forgetting that context switching between threads has overhead costs
